016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.rest.server.tracing.otel;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.StringUtils.*;
021import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Utils.*;
022
023import java.util.*;
024
025import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.*;
026import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.tracing.*;
027import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.tracing.Scope;
028
029import io.opentelemetry.api.*;
030import io.opentelemetry.api.common.*;
031import io.opentelemetry.api.trace.*;
032import io.opentelemetry.context.*;
033import io.opentelemetry.context.propagation.*;
034
035/**
036 * {@link TracerHook} bridge that opens an OpenTelemetry {@link Span} for every {@code @RestOp} call.
037 *
038 * <h5 class='topic'>Span shape</h5>
039 *
040 * <p>
041 * Each request becomes a single span with kind {@link SpanKind#SERVER}, named after the HTTP method
042 * (e.g. {@code "GET"}, {@code "POST"}) per the OpenTelemetry HTTP semantic conventions. Three OTel
043 * HTTP semantic-convention attributes are populated:
044 * <ul>
045 *    <li><b>{@code http.request.method}</b> &mdash; uppercased HTTP method.
046 *    <li><b>{@code http.response.status_code}</b> &mdash; HTTP response status (set right before the
047 *       span is closed, so error paths get the framework's resolved status).
048 *    <li><b>{@code http.route}</b> &mdash; the {@code @RestOp} path template (e.g.
049 *       {@code "/users/{id}"}), not the raw concrete URI. Keeps trace cardinality bounded for the same
050 *       reason the Micrometer bridge uses the template as its {@code uri} tag.
051 * </ul>
052 *
053 * <p>
054 * On the exception path, the throwable is recorded via {@link Span#recordException(Throwable)}, the
055 * span status is set to {@link StatusCode#ERROR}, and an {@code exception.type} attribute carries the
056 * exception's simple-name.
057 *
058 * <h5 class='topic'>W3C trace context propagation</h5>
059 *
060 * <p>
061 * Incoming {@code traceparent} / {@code tracestate} request headers are extracted via the configured
062 * {@link TextMapPropagator} (default: {@link io.opentelemetry.api.trace.propagation.W3CTraceContextPropagator}) so the server span continues
063 * a caller-supplied distributed trace. Downstream HTTP calls made from inside the handler will pick
064 * up the active span's context automatically when the consumer's outbound HTTP client honors the OTel
065 * {@link Context#current() current context} (the standard OTel client instrumentations do).
066 *
067 * <h5 class='topic'>Wiring</h5>
068 *
069 * <p class='bjava'>
070 *    <ja>@Configuration</ja>
071 *    <jk>public class</jk> ObservabilityConfig {
072 *
073 *       <jc>// Option A: rely on the JVM-wide GlobalOpenTelemetry (recommended).</jc>
074 *       <ja>@Bean</ja>
075 *       <jk>public</jk> TracerHook tracerHook() {
076 *          <jk>return new</jk> OtelTracerHook();
077 *       }
078 *
079 *       <jc>// Option B: pass a specific OpenTelemetry instance (e.g. for tests or multi-tenant setups).</jc>
080 *       <ja>@Bean</ja>
081 *       <jk>public</jk> TracerHook tracerHook(OpenTelemetry <jv>otel</jv>) {
082 *          <jk>return new</jk> OtelTracerHook(<jv>otel</jv>);
083 *       }
084 *    }
085 * </p>
086 *
087 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
088 *    <li class='jc'>{@link TracerHook}
089 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerObservability">REST Server &mdash; Observability (Micrometer + OpenTelemetry)</a>
090 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/http/http-spans/">OpenTelemetry HTTP semantic conventions</a>
091 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://www.w3.org/TR/trace-context/">W3C Trace Context</a>
092 * </ul>
093 *
094 * @since 10.0.0
095 */
096@SuppressWarnings({
097   "java:S115", // ARG_xxx constants use camelCase after prefix intentionally (constructor arg name keys, not enum-style constants)
098   "resource"   // Span / Scope returned to caller for try-with-resources management; Eclipse JDT @Owning warning is by design.
099})
100public class OtelTracerHook implements TracerHook {
101
102   /** Default tracer instrumentation scope name. */
103   public static final String DEFAULT_INSTRUMENTATION_NAME = "org.apache.juneau.rest.server";
104
105   /** OTel HTTP semantic-convention attribute key {@code http.request.method}. */
106   public static final AttributeKey<String> ATTR_HTTP_REQUEST_METHOD = AttributeKey.stringKey("http.request.method");
107
108   /** OTel HTTP semantic-convention attribute key {@code http.response.status_code}. */
109   public static final AttributeKey<Long> ATTR_HTTP_RESPONSE_STATUS_CODE = AttributeKey.longKey("http.response.status_code");
110
111   /** OTel HTTP semantic-convention attribute key {@code http.route}. */
112   public static final AttributeKey<String> ATTR_HTTP_ROUTE = AttributeKey.stringKey("http.route");
113
114   /** OTel attribute key {@code exception.type} used when recording a thrown exception. */
115   public static final AttributeKey<String> ATTR_EXCEPTION_TYPE = AttributeKey.stringKey("exception.type");
116
117   private static final String ARG_openTelemetry = "openTelemetry";
118   private static final String ARG_tracer = "tracer";
119   private static final String ARG_propagator = "propagator";
120
121   private final Tracer tracer;
122   private final TextMapPropagator propagator;
123
124   /**
125    * Constructor using {@link GlobalOpenTelemetry#get()} as the {@link OpenTelemetry} source.
126    *
127    * <p>
128    * Convenient when the deployment installs a single global {@link OpenTelemetry} (the dominant
129    * pattern with the OTel auto-instrumentation agent). Equivalent to
130    * {@code new OtelTracerHook(GlobalOpenTelemetry.get())}.
131    */
132   public OtelTracerHook() {
133      this(GlobalOpenTelemetry.get());
134   }
135
136   /**
137    * Constructor with an explicit {@link OpenTelemetry} instance.
138    *
139    * <p>
140    * The instance is used to obtain both the {@link Tracer} (named
141    * {@value #DEFAULT_INSTRUMENTATION_NAME}) and the {@link TextMapPropagator} used for incoming /
142    * outgoing W3C trace-context header transfer.
143    *
144    * @param openTelemetry The {@link OpenTelemetry} instance. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
145    */
146   public OtelTracerHook(OpenTelemetry openTelemetry) {
147      assertArgNotNull(ARG_openTelemetry, openTelemetry);
148      this.tracer = openTelemetry.getTracer(DEFAULT_INSTRUMENTATION_NAME);
149      this.propagator = openTelemetry.getPropagators().getTextMapPropagator();
150   }
151
152   /**
153    * Constructor with an explicit {@link Tracer} and {@link TextMapPropagator}.
154    *
155    * <p>
156    * Useful for tests where in-memory tracer / propagator instances are wired directly without going
157    * through a full {@link OpenTelemetry} SDK assembly.
158    *
159    * @param tracer The {@link Tracer} used to build per-request spans. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
160    * @param propagator The {@link TextMapPropagator} used for incoming / outgoing W3C trace-context
161    *    header transfer. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
162    */
163   public OtelTracerHook(Tracer tracer, TextMapPropagator propagator) {
164      this.tracer = assertArgNotNull(ARG_tracer, tracer);
165      this.propagator = assertArgNotNull(ARG_propagator, propagator);
166   }
167
168   @Override /* TracerHook */
169   public Scope startSpan(RestRequest request) {
170      Context extracted = propagator.extract(Context.current(), request, RestRequestTextMapGetter.INSTANCE);
171
172      String httpMethod = defaultIfBlank(request.getMethod(), "UNKNOWN");
173      String route = resolveRoute(request);
174
175      var spanBuilder = tracer.spanBuilder(httpMethod)
176         .setSpanKind(SpanKind.SERVER)
177         .setParent(extracted)
178         .setAttribute(ATTR_HTTP_REQUEST_METHOD, httpMethod);
179      if (! route.isEmpty())
180         spanBuilder.setAttribute(ATTR_HTTP_ROUTE, route);
181
182      Span span = spanBuilder.startSpan();
183      Context spanContext = extracted.with(span);
184
185      // Render the W3C trace-context headers from the server-started span's context (the only
186      // point where it's reliably active) and stash them as request attributes for
187      // TraceContextResponseProcessor to write on the response. The configured propagator only injects
188      // traceparent when the span context is valid, and tracestate only when non-empty, so both guards are
189      // handled here for free.
190      stashTraceContext(request, spanContext);
191
192      io.opentelemetry.context.Scope otelScope = spanContext.makeCurrent();
193
194      return new OtelScope(span, otelScope);
195   }
196
197   @Override /* TracerHook */
198   public Scope startSpan(String spanName) {
199      // Custom (non-request) observation: open an INTERNAL span parented to the current context
200      // (so it nests under an active request span when one exists), with no HTTP attributes.
201      Span span = tracer.spanBuilder(spanName)
202         .setSpanKind(SpanKind.INTERNAL)
203         .setParent(Context.current())
204         .startSpan();
205      io.opentelemetry.context.Scope otelScope = Context.current().with(span).makeCurrent();
206      return new OtelScope(span, otelScope);
207   }
208
209   private void stashTraceContext(RestRequest request, Context spanContext) {
210      var carrier = new HashMap<String,String>(4);
211      propagator.inject(spanContext, carrier, MAP_SETTER);
212      var traceparent = carrier.get("traceparent");
213      if (nn(traceparent))
214         request.setAttribute(TraceContextResponseProcessor.ATTR_TRACEPARENT, traceparent);
215      var tracestate = carrier.get("tracestate");
216      if (nn(tracestate) && ! tracestate.isEmpty())
217         request.setAttribute(TraceContextResponseProcessor.ATTR_TRACESTATE, tracestate);
218   }
219
220   private static final TextMapSetter<HashMap<String,String>> MAP_SETTER = (carrier, key, value) -> {
221      if (nn(carrier))
222         carrier.put(key, value);
223   };
224
225   /**
226    * Returns the {@link Tracer} this hook publishes spans to.
227    *
228    * @return The {@link Tracer}.
229    */
230   public Tracer getTracer() { return tracer; }
231
232   /**
233    * Returns the {@link TextMapPropagator} used to extract W3C trace-context headers from
234    * incoming requests.
235    *
236    * @return The {@link TextMapPropagator}.
237    */
238   public TextMapPropagator getPropagator() { return propagator; }
239
240   private static String resolveRoute(RestRequest request) {
241      try {
242         var opCtx = request.getOpContext();
243         return opCtx == null ? "" : defaultIfBlank(opCtx.getPathPattern(), "");
244      } catch (RuntimeException e) {
245         // Defensive: opCtx.getPathPattern() reaches into the path-matcher array; protect against NPE / array bounds in odd routing setups.
246         return "";
247      }
248   }
249
250   private static String defaultIfBlank(String value, String fallback) {
251      return isEmpty(value) ? fallback : value;
252   }
253
254   private static final class OtelScope implements Scope {
255
256      private final Span span;
257      private final io.opentelemetry.context.Scope scope;
258
259      OtelScope(Span span, io.opentelemetry.context.Scope scope) {
260         this.span = span;
261         this.scope = scope;
262      }
263
264      @Override /* Scope */
265      public void setStatusCode(int statusCode) {
266         span.setAttribute(ATTR_HTTP_RESPONSE_STATUS_CODE, (long) statusCode);
267         if (statusCode >= 500)
268            span.setStatus(StatusCode.ERROR);
269      }
270
271      @Override /* Scope */
272      public void setError(Throwable error) {
273         if (nn(error)) {
274            span.recordException(error);
275            span.setAttribute(ATTR_EXCEPTION_TYPE, cns(error));
276            span.setStatus(StatusCode.ERROR);
277         }
278      }
279
280      @Override /* Scope */
281      public void close() {
282         try {
283            scope.close();
284         } finally {
285            span.end();
286         }
287      }
288   }
289}
